rationale: The citing paper uses Rao (2022) as part of a central prior empirical stream on deceptive advertising and misinformation, not just as a passing citation. In the literature review, it explicitly groups Rao (2022) with studies that identify effects through regulator/platform bans and then argues that this approach leaves open whether direct exposure effects are symmetric to removal effects. That framing directly motivates the citing paper's experimental design and contribution on exposure and debunking effects.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the empirical line represented by Rao (2022) by moving from ban-based inference about misinformation effects to controlled direct exposure and debunking measurement. It builds on that prior approach to address a stated methodological gap in how misinformation effects are identified.
